hero.title: Turn Your Home Parking Into EV Charging
hero.body: If you have home parking with electricity, you can install EV charging at home. This lets you charge your EV safely and conveniently at your own place.
hero.trustPill: Massive Charging Network provides the charger and complete setup.

consideration.title: Things to Consider Before Installing EV Charging
consideration.subtitle: You don’t need to know everything — just a few basics:

cards.title: Parking Space
cards.text: How many EVs can be parked at your home? (1 car / 2 cars / more)

cards.title: Power Availability
cards.text: Existing electricity connection at your home vs Possibility of power upgrade.

cards.title: Type of EV
cards.text: 2-wheeler, 4-wheeler, or both?

cards.title: Usage Expectation
cards.text: Only for personal charging vs Family/Guests usage.

cards.title: Scalability
cards.text: Start small, upgrade or downgrade anytime. The setup grows with you.

consideration.ctaCard: 📌 Based on these factors, a single charging point or a small home charging station can be set up.

connection.title: Talk to Massive Charging Network
connection.subtitle: Fill in a few details and we’ll guide you.

hero.title: 🏠 EV Charging for Villa Owner
hero.body: If you have dedicated parking and access to electricity, you can set up EV charging here. This setup is designed for 4W and typically scales to 1–2 chargers.
hero.trustPill: Massive Charging Network provides the charger, installation, and compliance—optimized for smart ac charger.

consideration.title: Things to Consider for Villa Owner
consideration.subtitle: You don’t need to know everything — just a few basics:

cards.title: Parking & Layout
cards.text: Parking situation: Dedicated parking. Identify the most convenient bay/slot for cable routing and safe access.

cards.title: Power Availability
cards.text: Land situation: Private gated land. Check nearest power source, meter room access, and upgrade feasibility if needed.

cards.title: EV Type & Usage
cards.text: EV type: 4W. Estimate daily km / charging window to choose the right AC/DC setup.

cards.title: Safety & Compliance
cards.text: Use proper earthing, MCB/RCCB protection, and safe cable management for daily charging.

cards.title: Scale & Growth
cards.text: Planned scale: 1–2 chargers. Start with fewer points and expand with load management.

consideration.ctaCard: 📌 Based on these factors, we’ll recommend the right charger + wiring plan for villa owner.

connection.title: Talk to Massive Charging Network
connection.subtitle: Share a few details and we’ll guide you with the right setup.

hero.title: Install EV Charging in Your Parking Slot
hero.body: If you have an assigned parking slot with power, you can install EV charging. This removes dependence on public chargers.
hero.trustPill: Massive Charging Network provides compliant slot‑based charging solutions.

consideration.title: Things to Keep in Mind
consideration.subtitle: Check these basics before proceeding:

cards.title: Assigned Parking Availability
cards.text: Ensure you have a dedicated parking slot allocated to you.

cards.title: Society Permissions
cards.text: You may need approval or an NOC from your RWA/Management.

cards.title: Power Access at Slot
cards.text: Check if there is an existing power point or meter room nearby.

cards.title: EV Type and Daily Usage
cards.text: Determine if you need a simple socket (2W) or a fast charger (4W).

consideration.ctaCard: 👉 Check slot‑based charging feasibility.

hero.title: Set Up EV Charging for Your Society
hero.body: If your society has parking and electricity, you can set up EV charging. This supports residents and future EV adoption.
hero.trustPill: Massive Charging Network provides shared, scalable charging systems.

consideration.title: Things to Keep in Mind
consideration.subtitle: Key factors for society-wide implementation:

cards.title: Number of Parking Slots
cards.text: Assess total parking capacity to determine charging potential.

cards.title: Common Power Infrastructure
cards.text: Check availability of community power load for shared charging.

cards.title: Expected Resident EV Adoption
cards.text: Estimate how many residents plan to buy EVs soon.

cards.title: Shared vs Reserved Chargers
cards.text: Decide between dedicated slots or a common charging pool.

cards.title: Phased Deployment Possible
cards.text: You don't need to do it all at once; start small and scale up.

consideration.ctaCard: 👉 Assess EV charging readiness for your society.

consideration.title: Things to Keep in Mind

cards.text: Decide between a central hub or chargers at every cluster.

cards.title: Power Availability and Load Planning
cards.text: Ensure the grid can handle multiple chargers simultaneously.

cards.title: Long‑term Scalability
cards.text: Plan infrastructure that allows easy addition of future points.

hero.title: Add EV Charging at Your Location
hero.body: If vehicles stop at your site, you can offer EV charging. This prepares your business for the EV transition.
hero.trustPill: Massive Charging Network provides highway‑ready fast chargers.

consideration.title: Things to Keep in Mind
consideration.subtitle: Transitioning to the future of fuel:

cards.title: Stop Duration
cards.text: Highways require fast charging (DC) for quick turnaround.

cards.title: Highway Access
cards.text: Easy entry and exit from the main road is crucial.

cards.title: Power Availability
cards.text: High-tension connections are often needed for DC chargers.

cards.title: Fast‑charging Requirements
cards.text: Investing in higher capacity hardware for future-proofing.
